policy.scope: Validated Cyclades medium wind profiles only.
mediumToHigh: High is not granted by this audit alone.
High requires beach-specific trusted evidence: local/nautical/watersports/official/repeated observed behavior.
Geospatial-only medium profiles stay medium until external/local evidence is attached.
Known wind-sport high confidence can prove exposure/wind risk, not calm/protected swimming.

records.name: Vintzi
records.notes: Town/west-side Naxos pebble beach inferred from coordinates. Keep low confidence until exact cove exposure is verified. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Glatza
records.notes: Northwest/north Naxos rocky-pebble beach inferred from coordinates. Treat north/west-sector wind as exposed. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Klidos
records.notes: Southeast/east Naxos beach inferred from coordinates. Treat east/south-sector wind as exposed; no protected/calm claim. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Paralia Klido
records.notes: Southeast/east Naxos beach inferred from coordinates. East/south-sector wind remains the main caution. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Paralia Limnari
records.notes: East/northeast Naxos beach inferred from coordinates. North/east-sector wind should be handled as exposed until verified. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Melino
records.notes: North Naxos beach inferred from coordinates. Treat north-sector wind as exposed; no verified shelter claim. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Paralia Spedo
records.notes: Southeast/south Naxos beach inferred from coordinates. Treat east/south-sector wind as the main caution. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Chilia Vrysi
records.notes: North Naxos pebble/rock beach inferred from coordinates. Treat north/west-sector wind as exposed. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Paralia Psili Ammos
records.notes: East/southeast Naxos sandy beach inferred from coordinates and source metadata. East/south-sector wind remains a caution. Naxos wind-profile evidence gate 2026-06-08: verified OSM or verified nearby Nominatim identity, medium geospatial exposure, and no authored/geospatial sector conflict support medium confidence. This does not create a verified calm/protected claim.

records.name: Agia Irini
records.notes: Southwest Paros cove. Likely better in north-sector wind than open east/north beaches, but not a universal calm guarantee.

records.name: Agios Dimitrios
records.notes: North/northeast Paros beach with likely open fetch. North-sector wind can feel windy/choppy; avoid calm/protected claims in meltemi.

records.name: Agios Stefanos
records.notes: North Paros beach with likely N/NE exposure. Treat as wind-sensitive in 4-5 Bft unless local conditions prove otherwise.

records.name: Ampelas
records.notes: Northeast/east Paros beach with Aegean fetch. North/east-sector wind can create chop; do not promote as protected in meltemi.

records.name: Aspros Gremos
records.notes: East/northeast Paros exposure inferred from position. Use as wind-sensitive and avoid calm/protected wording in strong northerlies.

records.name: Voutakos
records.notes: Southwest Paros beach; should behave better than north/east exposed beaches in north wind, but bay shelter needs local check.

records.name: Glyfades
records.notes: East Paros open-fetch beach. North/east-sector wind can build chop quickly, so keep it out of calm-family claims in meltemi.

records.name: Delfini
records.notes: West Paros bay-side beach. Some local shelter may help, but protection is not verified enough for protected/calm claims.

records.name: Dryos
records.notes: Southeast Paros beach with directional exposure. It may behave differently from north-facing beaches, but exact shelter needs local check.

records.name: Kalogeros
records.notes: East Paros beach with open fetch toward the Aegean. NE/E wind can affect comfort; avoid protected/calm claims.

records.name: Krios
records.notes: Parikia bay area beach. Keep as semi-sheltered because bay shelter is not enough to make a verified north-wind protection claim.

records.name: Langeri
records.notes: North/northeast Paros beach with likely open exposure. Strong north-sector wind can be uncomfortable for casual swimming.

records.name: Livadia
records.notes: Parikia bay beach. Treat as partly sheltered only; do not create protected-from-north or calm-water claims from this profile.

records.name: Logaras
records.notes: Southeast Paros beach. It can differ from north-exposed beaches, but exact local shelter is not verified enough for protected claims.

records.name: Lolantonis
records.notes: South-facing Paros beach; likely a better north-wind swimming option than open east/north beaches. South-sector winds are the caution.

records.name: Martselo
records.notes: Parikia bay beach with partial bay influence. Keep as semi-sheltered and avoid north-wind protection claims without local verification.

records.name: Mikri Santa Maria
records.notes: Northeast Paros beach with open fetch; north-sector wind can feel breezy/choppy. Do not sell as protected in meltemi.

records.name: Molos
records.notes: Open east-side bay facing toward Naxos. North/east-sector wind can build chop quickly; not a calm protected pick in meltemi.

records.name: Navtikos omilos Parou
records.notes: Parikia waterfront/bay area. Urban bay position may reduce fetch, but not enough for a verified protected/calm label.

records.name: Xyfara
records.notes: Northeast Paros beach with likely Aegean exposure. North/east-sector wind can make it choppy; avoid calm/protected claims.

records.name: Paralia Agkali Chrysis Aktis
records.notes: Part of the Golden Beach wind/watersports cluster. Treat like high-exposure in 4-5 Bft; separate from calmer south-coast alternatives.

records.name: Paralia Glyfa
records.notes: South Paros beach with some north-wind lee effect. Keep as semi-sheltered because exact cove protection needs local verification.

records.name: Paralia Parikias
records.notes: Parikia bay beach. Use as semi-sheltered only; the profile must not imply verified protection from north wind.

records.name: Parasporos
records.notes: Southwest Paros beach that may be more practical than open north/east beaches, but local protection still needs verification.

records.name: Piperi
records.notes: Naousa bay area beach. Keep as semi-sheltered because exact cove shelter is not verified for north-wind calm claims.

records.name: Pounta
records.notes: Known windy/kite area; should not be promoted as calm with 4-5 Beaufort.
blockers: Pounta/Punda identity ambiguity: app coordinates match the east/southeast Punda Coast beach, while many kite references describe the west Pounda channel opposite Antiparos.

records.name: Pyrgaki
records.notes: South Paros beach. It may perform differently from north-exposed beaches, but use cautious wording until local shelter is verified.

records.name: Santa Maria
records.notes: Northeast Paros beach with open Aegean fetch. North-sector wind can be uncomfortable for relaxed swimming.

records.name: Tourkou Ammos
records.notes: North Paros beach with direct north-sector exposure. Strong northerlies can be uncomfortable; do not label as protected.

records.name: Agios Sostis
records.notes: South-coast Milos beach near the Provatas zone. Likely more manageable than open north-coast beaches in north wind, but not verified enough for calm/protected claims.

records.name: Agia Kyriaki
records.notes: South/southeast Milos beach. Often a better north-wind candidate than north-coast beaches, while east/south-sector wind can still affect comfort.

records.name: Palaiochori
records.notes: South/east Milos beach. Likely a better candidate in north wind than open north-coast beaches, but south/east winds are the local caution.

records.name: Papafragkas
records.notes: North-coast cave/cove beach. Pocket shelter is possible, but north-sector waves or surge can enter; never treat as simply calm/protected in meltemi.

records.name: Plathiena
records.notes: Northwest/north Milos beach. Meltemi can be a direct comfort issue; south/southwest wind is usually less onshore here, but this is not a guaranteed calm-water claim.

records.name: Provatas
records.notes: Main south-coast Milos option. Often more practical in north wind than exposed northern beaches, but south-sector wind can reverse the recommendation.

records.name: Sarakiniko
records.notes: Sarakiniko is a high-impact north-coast sightseeing beach. Do not treat it as calm/protected in meltemi; south/southwest wind is usually less onshore here, but recommend only when sea and wind data support swimming.

records.name: Tourkothalassa
records.notes: North/northwest Milos beach. Treat as meltemi-sensitive and avoid calm-family recommendations in 4-5 Bft north-sector wind; south/southwest wind is generally less onshore here.

records.name: Tsigkrado
records.notes: South-coast cove that may be better in north wind. Access is difficult and wind shelter must not be interpreted as safe swimming in poor sea conditions.

records.name: Fyriplaka
records.notes: Popular south-coast Milos beach. Likely a better north-wind option than open northern beaches, but do not oversell it in south/east wind.

records.name: Fyropotamos
records.notes: North-coast village bay. Beautiful and practical in some conditions, but should not be sold as calm/protected during meltemi; southwest wind is generally less direct than north-sector wind.

records.name: Psarovolada
records.notes: Southeast Milos beach near the Agia Kyriaki/Palaiochori zone. Likely better in north wind, but east/south-sector wind remains a caution.

records.name: Ag.Kyprianos
records.notes: West Andros practical beach near Gavrio/Batsi. Better than open east bays in NE wind, but not verified as protected from due north.

records.name: Agios Ioannis
records.notes: South/east Andros beach with possible lee from north-sector wind, but protection is not verified enough for protected/calm claims.

records.name: Agios Petros
records.notes: Open west-coast sandy beach. Often more practical than east-coast bays in NE wind, but can still feel breezy/choppy with N/NW.

records.name: Apothikes
records.notes: South-coast beach; should behave differently from north-exposed beaches in north wind, but remains exposed with S/SW wind.

records.name: Ateni
records.notes: East/northeast Andros beach. North-sector wind can create uncomfortable chop quickly; avoid protected/calm claims.

records.name: Achla
records.notes: Open east-coast Andros beach with long Aegean fetch. Strong north/east-sector winds can make swimming less comfortable.

records.name: Vitali
records.notes: North/east-facing Andros beach. Meltemi can arrive directly and build chop; not a calm-family fallback in 4-5 Bft.

records.name: Vlychada
records.notes: Northwest Andros open-fetch beach. North/west-sector wind can build chop quickly; avoid calm/protected wording.

records.name: Vori
records.notes: Open north/east Andros beach. High fetch in north-sector wind; use caution for casual swimming during meltemi.

records.name: Vintzi
records.notes: South Andros/Korthi-side beach. It may differ from north-exposed beaches, but exact shelter is not verified enough for protected claims.

records.name: Gyalia
records.notes: Chora/east-side Andros beach with likely north/east exposure. Do not promote as calm/protected in meltemi.

records.name: Zorkos
records.notes: Large northern Andros beach with open fetch. North wind can be a direct comfort problem; avoid calm/protected wording.

records.name: Kalamitsi
records.notes: Northwest Andros beach with open fetch. North/west-sector winds can make casual swimming uncomfortable.

records.name: Kaminaki
records.notes: Northwest Andros beach near other exposed coves. Treat as wind-sensitive in 4-5 Bft northerlies.

records.name: Kolona
records.notes: Small Batsi-area beach. Practical leeward alternative in some east/northeast winds, but not confirmed protected from due north.

records.name: Kourtali
records.notes: Northwest/west Andros open-fetch beach. Strong north/west-sector winds can be uncomfortable for relaxed swimming.

records.name: Kypri
records.notes: West-coast practical beach. Often preferable to open east/north bays in NE wind, but N/NW wind can still be felt.

records.name: Lydi
records.notes: Southeast/east Andros beach. May differ from north-exposed beaches, but exact shelter is not verified enough for protected claims.

records.name: Batsi
records.notes: Batsi bay area; more practical in north/east-sector wind than exposed east beaches, though not guaranteed flat everywhere in the bay.

records.name: Bouros
records.notes: South Andros beach. It may be more practical than open north/east beaches in north wind, but not verified as fully sheltered.

records.name: Palaiopoli
records.notes: West/southwest Andros beach. Often behaves differently from open east beaches, but do not use this as a north-protection claim.

records.name: Paralia Felou
records.notes: Northwest Andros beach with open-sector exposure. North/west winds can make it choppy; avoid protected/calm wording.

records.name: Paraporti
records.notes: Chora-side Andros beach with likely wind exposure. Treat as wind-sensitive and do not claim north-wind protection.

records.name: Piso Gyalia
records.notes: More sheltered Andros option; usually better than open north/east bays during north-sector wind. Not a universal calm guarantee.

records.name: Piso Limnionas
records.notes: Northwest Andros cove with open-fetch risk. North/west-sector wind can be uncomfortable for casual swimming.

records.name: Pyrgos
records.notes: Northwest Andros beach with likely open fetch. Strong north/west-sector wind should be treated as a swimming comfort risk.

records.name: Syneti
records.notes: Open east-side bay; north-sector wind can build chop/waves quickly. Do not claim protected in meltemi.

records.name: Tis grias to pidima
records.notes: Korthi/southeast Andros beach. It may differ from north-exposed beaches, but gusts and local exposure still need caution.

records.name: Chrysi Ammos
records.notes: Popular west-coast Andros beach. Better than east open bays in NE wind, but still not a verified sheltered cove for due north wind.

records.name: Agia Kyriaki
records.notes: Medium-confidence geospatial backfill from validated island orientation, sector exposure, fetch, and shoreline-segment stability. This does not grant high confidence or guaranteed calm/protected claims.
